item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a great read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Isn't this based off a creepypasta, all SCPs are original ideas right (except SCP-1000 and this concept)? Other than that this is really cool

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
why do i like this skip so much???

it's a very simple article, but theres just something about it!!

maybe its the mental images made by the instances of 4136-2, or just how funny the concept of "my uncle who works at nintendo" being a mysterious entity that gives children fucked up nintendo systems is, or the fact that i havent read that many series v articles, but this is definitely my favorite series v article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Doesn't do it for me. I like the retro feel but I think the content is without a narrative hint, it's descriptive in a good way but needs more "meat".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
my uncle actually works at nintendo so your post kinda offends me he would never do this to me. but also he said that goku is going to be in smash ultimate and if you dont see him you just have to get the secret maximum score in home run contest and nobody has ever done it except me once but i accidentally deleted the save file.

But other than that, really good SCP, +1 for sure

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be honest, I though this was going for the route of “My uncle works at Nintendo so don’t mess with me” when I read the title, but I like this better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: uncleo dados brother he make good game 4 nefew

item.title: Re: Physics is wrong
item.content:encoded:
Now it is "+"

Without Neptune it is much more plausible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Um, no, it wasn’t — this was made independent of any creepy pastas that may or may not have a similar premise to my article. I’ve heard of a game with a similar premise but I’ve never played it or watched a let’s play of it.

If you think the article’s top similar to other pieces, that’s fine, comes with the territory. However, I’d prefer you not insinuate that I basically took a creepy pasta and made an SCP from it.

(Sent from my iPhone)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not a coincidence. This entire post was written to put that creepypasta into the format of an SCP.

item.title: uncle
item.content:encoded: oh its me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For a second, I thought dado had something to do with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually like this a lot more, it's actually pretty creepy in some parts. Good job.

item.title: Re: Physics is wrong
item.content:encoded: I'd like to just think that the object can just mess with physics — although yeah I can understand how weird it is. I'll edit it around.

item.title: Physics is wrong
item.content:encoded:
Targeted tissue becomes weightless and floats in the air.

and

Bodies are believed to have just entered the orbit of Neptune.

Those two quotes indicate major physics screw-up.

  1. Escape velocity from Earth is about 11.1 km/s, distance from Earth to Neptune is around 4.3*109 km at its closest, which gives average speed ~= 4.13 km/s. Drop of speed is unexplained;
  2. Adiabatic compression will incinerate human if it launched with escape velocity;
  3. Transition from "floats in the air" to escape velocity is unexplained.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Subjects affected by SCP-4136-1 report that SCP-4136-1

I believe you made a mistake here, but commenting just to be sure. >.>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've never read that creepypasta, so I can't say how similar this is to that — I know there's a game called this, but I haven't played it.

If it makes things better, this was a rewrite of an article written by Roget two years ago. Not sure if it does, but just thought I'd put that out there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to have to no-vote. Maybe it's just a coincidence, but there is a creepypasta called 'My Uncle Who Works for Nintendo' and it has quite a few similarities.

That said, it was a well-written and the twists at the end were pretty good, it is just that I can't put off the thought of 'My Uncle Who Works for Nintendo'.

No-vote for now, but maybe will change my mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You wrote the same description of the super scope twice btw

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A bit on the nose, but as someone who grew up with this sort of thing and can remember the pre and early internet era of random vg speculation this spoke to me.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This is a rewrite of SCP-2921 (" My Uncle Who Works At Nintendo"), written by Roget on December 24 2016. This has been a rewrite like….two years in the making, almost.1 This went through various versions until I got onto this, with a lot of help from critics.

Thank to Othellocat, Hippo, and amnestic_protocol for looking at recent versions of this. Thanks to Roget for writing the original, and for early, early critiques on a much cruder version of the article.

+ Original
- Hide whatever

Item #: SCP-2916

Object Class: Euclid

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-2916 materials are to be stored in Site-77's anomalous storage wing. Foundation agents embedded in the USPS and other delivery services are to be given regular instructions on how to recover SCP-2916, should they discover any, in their standard briefings and operational guidelines.

Affected subjects are to be issued Class-B to -A amnestics depending on how long they have been affected. Subjects proving resistant to amnestics may be held indefinitely within the Site-19 detention facilities for further study.

Description: SCP-2916 is an infohazardous anomalous phenomenon affecting adolescent humans. Subjects affected by SCP-2916 are most easily identifiable by the delusion that they have an uncle who works for the Nintendo of America corporation.

The vector for infection is unknown, however approximately 2,000 subjects affected by SCP-2916 have been identified since 1999. Over time, subjects develop false memories regarding the non-existent individual which can cause cognitive dissonance and emotional distress. Relatives and friends of the affected subject may also be affected, the likelihood of being affected appears to be related to the potential infected's willpower and willingness to give affected subjects the benefit of the doubt.

SCP-2916 subjects frequently experience reoccurring nightmares, in which they are awoken from their sleep and wander through their home before discovering their 'uncle' staring at an active CRT-style television. Descriptions of this individual have been inconsistent, although it has always described as being male, bald, and wearing highly reflective glasses.

After an unknown period of time after being affected, the subjects will begin to receive anomalously generated packages and letters through the United States Postal Service. Although delivery records show USPS employees delivering the mail, no specific employees have been identified. No delivery of SCP-2916 materials has been directly witnessed by Foundation personnel.

Some materials delivered to SCP-2916 include:

  • A copy of the manual for the game "Duck Hunt", identical to a normal manual, with the 'Notes' section containing detailed, hand-written instructions on how the message's recipient could obtain a firearm from a relative to go hunting with.
  • A photograph, badly deteriorated, showing a group of people sitting behind a bar, raising glasses. A hand-written note on the back read "Here with the folks at NINTENDO!"
  • The Sega-manufactured light game gun "Justifier" with dried bloodstains on the handle; A note enclosed with the object insisted that it would work on a Nintendo console and to "ignore the smell!".
  • A strategy guide for the game "Castlevania II: Simon's Quest" in a package also containing a bundle of white bandages that smelled of decomposition. Bandages contained only ashes.

During testing, a blank sheet of paper recovered with a subject affected by SCP-2916 was placed in water, and as a result previously unknown text was discovered. The content of the letter has been included in this documentation.

Dear Steve
So sorry I didn't write you sooner! I've just gotten home from another long session of working at Nintendo! You're turning out to be a fine young man. I was very pleased to see you write me. I'm sorry to hear about your setbacks at home. I saddened me when you wrote about having so many of your father's habits.
I think it's a great idea for you to visit me during vacation. You'll enjoy being with me, here at Nintendo. We can do so much with people like you, and I really think you'll like everyone here. They're dying to meet you, I've told them all about you.
Hope to see you soon and give your games my regards,

Uncle

Footnotes
1. sorry for the wait roget
